title: Delicate Mexican Redcurrants Horchata Bowl
description: A refreshing, sour horchata bowl with lightly fried redcurrants, perfect for a quick weeknight treat.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine coconut milk and warm water, heat over medium until warm but not boiling.
Whisk in almond flour, ground cinnamon, vanilla extract, and sea salt until the mixture is smooth and free of lumps.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool to room temperature, then chill it in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to set.
While the horchata chills, rinse the redcurrants under cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Toss the redcurrants with chili powder, lime zest, and a pinch of crushed peppercorns until they are evenly coated.
Heat a small nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, add a splash of coconut oil, and swirl to coat the surface.
Add the seasoned redcurrants to the skillet and sear them for 2 minutes, flipping once, until they develop a slight char and soften.
To serve, pour chilled horchata into bowls and top with the seared redcurrants, then drizzle each bowl with fresh lime juice.
Garnish with a few extra lime zest curls and a sprinkle of ground cinnamon for an extra burst of flavor.
